transcript.clean_text: the last day of road racing ahead of the time trial on sunday stage 20 of love welter was spectacular indeed beautiful too the home of our race director and he chose one heck of a course climbing straight out of the back door not categorized you understand but some real inclined tests and with over 4 200 metres of altitude gain on the day and the categorized climbs coming in the second half it was going to draw a breakaway and a spectacular one we had well they posted such a phenomenal gap of nearly 12 minutes everyone believed they would go all the way well the chase was a half-hearted one from jumbo visma and so it was that inies grenadiers came to the fore and slowly but surely as the mountains points went the way of michael storer to make him certain of that king of the mountains jersey the gap was closing well with 78 kilometers and more to go ines grenadiers were sacrificing themselves splits were happening there was riders all over the mountain and even india's grenadiers themselves were losing out in terms of manpower don't forget three riders down coming into today egan bernard was the man who was calling for pace but it was adam yates that was setting it the way he went and it got very selective the chase and in so doing almost automatically it closed the gap on the break which itself was fracturing the red jersey group still included bernal and roglich and bernal himself was setting out it was a tough day well the tempo had been set by the demands of igan bernal but ultimately he was to be something of a loser on the day adam yates struck out roglich wanted to follow this was a move that bernal did not chase down mass was there as well ultimately and we were setting ourselves up for a grandstand finale well this group with bernal started to drift back and accepted miguel anger lopez as part of its membership ultimately lopez was to step off the bike on this day but one rider from the break was determined to take control of it away he went on a major descent off down category one test the mugas the alto de prado beckoned and of course ultimately the castro de lv well riders seem to have lost much of the heart for the chase and some were setting up there was a regroup one down there but the big gaps were starting to show and in the virtual classification roglich leading mass hague onto the podium yates in fourth place amigolanga lopez dropping like a stone ultimately as we said off the bike out front gibbons was giving it his all but ramps of 14 and more were starting to take a toll and when we came to our final climb a significant section of two kilometers was waiting to hurt him still brave and still with a margin of a minute and 15 he pushed on on his tail out of a a bunch of favorites now bizkara rolling the dice for the last time hoping for a spanish victory which has evaded spain and spanish riders throughout all of the grand tours this year yates bridged over to bizcarra and went for it hague now alone having burnt out gino maeda and indeed mark pardon who'd been in the break and what a quartet this turned out to be the favorites for the race in fact the one two three four at the end of the day as far as the overall standing is concerned and they were charging over those inclines haig the man that was suffering and finally they caught gibbons well for all intents and purposes with 3.4 kilometers to go we thought that the winner would come certainly from this group either gibbons himself are one of the four favorites well we thought without the sprightly nature of a few others clermont chapel zone left it very very late after bisgara had rolled the dice earlier and he went for it 1700 meters to go wrong glitch and co just looked on at his back wheel wondering who would take up the chase and in so doing potentially lead somebody else to glory chapeza emptied himself and when he reached the final crest he couldn't quite believe it he had to add heart on this day and he had plenty roglic chasing on for some bonus seconds to extend his lead adam yates coming home in third ahead of mass and hague chris hamilton who was part of a three-man section of that breakaway coming home next but some big gaps there were an ag2r citroen finally got themselves a stage win boy had they deserved it they've done so much work in this race and now finally a stage win to their name it was a wonderful day of racing chapel to chapel for never giving up roglic yates mass hague hamilton biscara gibbons maida who moved into the white jersey and the tear so primos roglic it is that leads this race going into the grand finale the time trial to santiago de compostela an enhanced margin of 2 minutes and 38 over enrique mass jack hagan third place head of adam yates and the new leader of the white jersey competition gino maeda who's done so much work for jack hague to help him onto that podium king of the mountain it will be michael store with no mountains points tomorrow he just has to ride through the course wonderful result for him as well as two stage wins for himself and one for bar day for that team dsm taking a bow gino maida you're going to see a lot more of him overtakes iguan bernal in the young riders competition and indeed leads it by a margin of 3 minutes and 24 that was impressive for an impressive rider and so to stage 21 santiago de compostela beckons it's a wonderful destination top and tailed by a race between cathedrals this year and a time trial to decide all of the final placings 33.8 kilometers in total slight lumpy cause to it and roglitch the king in waiting so the mighty cathedral beckons as our finishing point tomorrow say your prayers for everybody a wonderful race this has been and it will be the cherry on the cake as they take on the battle against the clock we'll see you then
